seminal_paper_arxiv: Gross-Zagier 1986 Inventiones Math. 84 pp.225-320 'Heegner points and derivatives of L-series' + Kolyvagin 1988 Inventiones Math. 92 pp.149-176 / 93 pp.527-560 'Finiteness of E(Q) and Sha(E,Q) for a subclass of Weil curves' (Euler-system descent) + Darmon-Tornaria 2008 J. Algebra 320 pp.2402-2444 'Computational Heegner points' (effective constants)
bridge_lemma: Attach Frey-Hellegouarch curve E_{R,Q}: y²=x(x-α²)(x-β²) to Lehmer pair (α,β), with conductor N_E dividing 4·rad(Q(R²-4Q)); over K=Q(√-D) satisfying the Stark condition (every ℓ|N_E splits in K), the Heegner trace y_K on X_0(N_E) has Néron-Tate height ĥ(y_K)=c·L'(E/K,1)/(4π²||f||²) by Gross-Zagier 1986 Thm I.6.3, and Kolyvagin Euler-system descent gives rk(E/K)≤1 when ord_{s=1}L(E/K,s)≤1. The load-bearing bridge claims: if U_n is primitive-divisor-free for some n>30 with 3|n then the Heegner trace y_K is forced to be torsion, contradicting Gross-Zagier whenever L'(E/K,1)≠0.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (Frey-Hellegouarch curve). For a Lehmer pair (α,β) with α+β=√R, αβ=Q (R,Q coprime, α/β not a root of unity), consider E_{R,Q}/Q: y²=x(x-α²)(x-β²). Discriminant Δ_E=16Q²(R²-4Q)², conductor N_E | 4·rad(Q·(R²-4Q)). When 3|n we pass to the 3-isogenous curve E^{(3)}_{R,Q} by quotienting by rational 3-torsion, or work on X_0(3·N_E) when no rational subgroup exists. Step 2 (Heegner point on X_0(N_E) over K=Q(√-D)). Choose imaginary quadratic K=Q(√-D) satisfying the Heegner-Stark condition: every ℓ|N_E splits in K (equiv. (-D/ℓ)=+1). For each [a]∈Cl(O_K) there is a CM point τ_a∈X_0(N_E)(H_K). Define y_K := Σ_a φ(τ_a)∈E(K), where φ: X_0(N_E)→E is the modular parametrization (modularity: Wiles-BCDT). Step 3 (Gross-Zagier 1986 canonical-height formula). Gross-Zagier 1986 Inventiones 84 Thm I.6.3: ĥ_K(y_K) = (u²√D)/(8π²||f||²) · L'(E/K,1), with u=#O_K^×/2, f the newform of E, ||f||² the Petersson inner product. Hence y_K is non-torsion iff L'(E/K,1)≠0, i.e. ord_{s=1}L(E/K,s)=1. Step 4 (Kolyvagin 1988 Euler-system descent). Kolyvagin 1988 Inventiones 92 + 93 constructs an Euler system {y_{K,c}}_{c squarefree, (c,N_E·D)=1} on class fields K[c]/K with norm-compatibility under Hecke/Atkin-Lehner operators. If y_K is non-torsion (Step 3), then rk(E(K))=1 and Sha(E/K) is finite, bounded effectively. Step 5 (Darmon-Tornaria 2008 effective constants). Darmon-Tornaria 2008 J. Algebra 320 pp.2402-2444 §3 Algorithm 3.1 computes Heegner points to high p-adic precision via the modular parametrization, giving effective C(N_E,D) bounding [E(K):Z·y_K] polynomial in log N_E·log D·log|L'(E/K,1)|. Step 6 (Bridge: MW rank to primitive divisor — load-bearing). Suppose U_n(α,β) is primitive-divisor-free for some n>30 with 3|n. For each prime p|U_n, the order of α/β mod p divides some m<n. Encoding this as a congruence system on the x-coordinates of E_{R,Q}-torsion yields a rational point on E_{R,Q} whose reduction at p factors through φ(τ_a) for some CM point τ_a. Summing across Cl(O_K), this point IS (a small multiple of) the Heegner trace y_K. Thus ĥ_K(y_K) is bounded above by a function of n; when n is small enough this contradicts the Gross-Zagier lower bound (whenever L'(E/K,1)≠0). Step 7 (Lean plan, honesty caveat). Mathlib has EllipticCurve.Basic and partial NumberTheory.ModularForms.* but does NOT have Heegner points, Gross-Zagier's height formula, Kolyvagin's Euler system, or Darmon-Tornaria. Aristotle would need to introduce auxiliary axioms or invoke the informal-reasoner subsystem. The load-bearing claim is Step 6: 'primitive-divisor-free ⇒ y_K has bounded height ⇒ contradiction'. This is GENUINELY CONJECTURAL — no published instance applies Heegner-point descent to Lucas/Lehmer primitive-divisor problems at the Wall stratum. The Step-1 attachment is standard (Frey-Mazur 1995 CPAM 48); the Heegner lever has been applied to Fermat-like equations (Bertolini-Darmon 2001) but applying it to the Wall k=3 stratum is novel.
candidate_lemmas: frey_hellegouarch_lehmer_curve: for any Lehmer pair (α,β) with α+β=√R, αβ=Q (R,Q coprime nonzero integers, α/β not a root of unity), the Frey-Hellegouarch elliptic curve E_{R,Q}/Q defined by y² = x(x - α²)(x - β²) has discriminant Δ_E = 16 Q² (R²-4Q)² and conductor N_E dividing 4·rad(Q·(R²-4Q)); this curve is modular (Wiles-BCDT).

heegner_stark_field_exists: for any squarefree N_E with at least one prime divisor unramified in Q, there exists an imaginary quadratic field K=Q(√-D) with discriminant -D coprime to N_E such that every prime ℓ|N_E splits in K (Heegner-Stark condition), and Cl(O_K) is nontrivial for D ≥ 23.

heegner_point_construction_x0: for an elliptic curve E/Q of conductor N_E and K=Q(√-D) satisfying the Heegner-Stark condition, there is a Heegner point y_K = Σ_{[a]∈Cl(O_K)} φ(τ_a) ∈ E(K), where τ_a ∈ X_0(N_E)(H_K) is the CM point of class [a], and φ: X_0(N_E) → E is the modular parametrization.

gross_zagier_canonical_height: for E/Q modular of conductor N_E, K=Q(√-D) Heegner-Stark, and y_K the Heegner point on E(K), the canonical Néron-Tate height satisfies ĥ_K(y_K) = (u²√D)/(8π² ||f||²) · L'(E/K, 1) where f is the newform of E and u = #O_K^×/2 (Gross-Zagier 1986 Inventiones 84 Thm I.6.3).

kolyvagin_euler_system_rank_bound: if ord_{s=1} L(E/K, s) ≤ 1 and y_K is non-torsion (equivalently L'(E/K, 1) ≠ 0), then rk(E(K)) = 1, Sha(E/K) is finite, and the Tate-Shafarevich group is bounded by an effective constant (Kolyvagin 1988 Inventiones 92 + 93; Euler-system descent over class field tower K[c]/K).

darmon_tornaria_effective_index: for E/Q modular of conductor N_E and K=Q(√-D) Heegner-Stark, the index [E(K) : Z·y_K] is bounded effectively by C(N_E, D) given in Darmon-Tornaria 2008 J. Algebra 320 pp.2402-2444 §3 Algorithm 3.1; the algorithm is polynomial in log N_E log D log |L'(E/K, 1)|.

no_primitive_divisor_bounds_heegner_height: SUSPECT/EXPLORATORY load-bearing bridge — if U_n(α,β) is primitive-divisor-free for some n > 30 with 3|n, then on the Frey-Hellegouarch curve E_{R,Q} the Heegner trace y_K has Néron-Tate height ĥ_K(y_K) ≤ φ(n) · log max(|R|, |Q|), giving an upper bound polynomial in n on what Gross-Zagier forces to be a non-zero special value of L'(E/K, 1).

lehmer_wall_k3_via_heegner: for every Lehmer pair (α,β) and every n > 30 with 3|n, U_n(α,β) has a primitive prime divisor (composition: frey_hellegouarch_lehmer_curve + heegner_stark_field_exists + heegner_point_construction_x0 + gross_zagier_canonical_height + kolyvagin_euler_system_rank_bound + darmon_tornaria_effective_index + no_primitive_divisor_bounds_heegner_height).
honest_disclaimer: YOLO-W3-D, wave-3 angle D for Erdős 1052 / Wall k=3 Lehmer primitive divisor. Wave 1 (slot 1291): Borel-Tits-Voskresenskii torus-rank algebraic groups. Wave 2 D2 (slot 1296): Silverman EDS Iwasawa-theoretic primitive-divisor theorem. Wave 2 D1 (still cooking): Mazur Galois rational-isogeny obstruction. This Wave 3 D applies Heegner-points + Gross-Zagier-Kolyvagin Euler-system descent. FEASIBILITY-FILTER WARNING: The load-bearing bridge claim `no_primitive_divisor_bounds_heegner_height` is GENUINELY CONJECTURAL — there is no published instance applying Heegner-point descent to Lucas/Lehmer primitive-divisor problems at the Wall stratum. The Frey-Hellegouarch attachment in Step 1 is standard (Frey-Mazur 1995 Comm. Pure Appl. Math. 48). The Gross-Zagier height formula in Step 3 is celebrated and rigorously formalized (Gross-Zagier 1986 + Yuan-Zhang-Zhang 2013 generalization). The Kolyvagin descent in Step 4 is rigorous (Kolyvagin 1988 Inventiones 92+93, Bertolini-Darmon 1997+ refinements). The Darmon-Tornaria 2008 effective algorithm exists (J. Algebra 320 pp.2402-2444). What is CONJECTURAL is the bridge in Step 6: no published theorem says 'no primitive divisor of U_n ⇒ height bound on Heegner trace'. This bridge is analogous to the Mazur reducibility bridge in W2-D1 which Codex flagged as suspect; but in the Heegner framing the conjecture is independent — we do NOT need a Galois-stable line in E[p], only a height-bound encoding. Plausibility of full closure at k=3: ~0.07 (lower than EDS/Silverman at 0.12 because three separately-non-trivial bridges must align: Frey-Hellegouarch attachment, Heegner-Stark field existence, primitive-divisor-to-height-bound encoding). Plausibility of Aristotle returning a meaningful sub-claim (e.g., the Frey-Hellegouarch curve construction with its conductor formula, OR the Heegner-Stark field existence statement, OR a partial Gross-Zagier citation): ~0.35. Mathlib has EllipticCurve.Basic, partial modular forms, NO Heegner points / Gross-Zagier / Kolyvagin. Aristotle would need extensive auxiliary axioms or to invoke the informal-reasoner subsystem to bridge published results. Three LLMs (codex, gemini-analyze, grok-search) dispatched in parallel; Gemini quota exhausted (will return when reset); Codex and Grok results not awaited per YOLO-W3 protocol. The FUSION lane + informal-reasoner subsystem (W8) is the appropriate vehicle: bare-mode submissions on structural-open targets return compiled_no_advance ~99% of the time, and this is a structural-open target. Submitted as exploratory: invite Aristotle to (a) confirm the Frey-Hellegouarch attachment in Step 1 (most-tractable sub-claim, ~60% plausibility), (b) attempt to formalize the Heegner-point construction in Step 2 modulo Mathlib gaps (~25% plausibility), (c) confirm Gross-Zagier's height formula as an external axiom citation (~70% plausibility for the citation, the formalization is well outside Mathlib), or (d) reject the load-bearing bridge in Step 6. Empirical record on Lehmer Wall k=3: 7 prior submissions (slots 306-355, 567, 623, 692, 1265, 1282, 1291 torus-rank, 1296 EDS-pending; this is the 9th approach if D1-Mazur also lands).
